topic: Working-tabs state machine + request data model (RequestSpec) for an HTTP client, built as a zustand tabs slice with a TabBar strip UI
verbatim_prompt: The working-tabs state machine and request data model for an HTTP client. Each open tab holds a RequestSpec: { method, url, name, params[], headers[], body: {lang, type, text}, auth }, where each param/header row is { enabled, key, value, description }. auth is a discriminated union keyed on `type`; T4 defines ONLY the shape and the two variants it actually needs — { type: 'none' } and { type: 'bearer', token } — and leaves auth intentionally extensible: the full set of auth types and their per-type fields (basic user/pass, api-key with name+in, oauth2, etc.) is owned by the auth-panel task and is out of scope here. auth is the SINGLE source of truth for authorization — do NOT also seed an Authorization row into headers[]; turning auth into an actual wire header is the http-engine's job, not duplicated tab state. Tab lifecycle: open-from-collection (dedupe by id then url), new blank tab, close (never leave zero tabs — closing the last spawns a fresh Untitled GET), select-active. A dirty flag flips on any edit; T4 also exposes a markClean(tabId) action but does NOT own its triggers — a successful send (http-engine task) or save (persistence task) calls markClean, both out of scope here, so what T4 implements and tests is the flag plus the action, never a real send. New tabs seed auth = { type: 'bearer', token: '{{apiKey}}' } (the literal {{apiKey}} string is stored verbatim; variable interpolation is a separate task) and headers[] with a default Accept row. Implemented as a zustand tabs slice with a TabBar strip as UI; the request-editing panels are out of scope. Visual reference for the TabBar: design/reference.html (a Claude Design export) — use it for look and behavior only, never reproduce its markup; it carries generated cruft (data-om-* attributes, __OmT wrappers, inline styles, a tweaks-panel) that must not enter the build. Rebuild with semantic class names from role and tag function. Colors come from design/tokens.json.

functional_scope.value: A zustand tabs slice owning the open-request tabs + the lifecycle state machine (open-from-collection with dedupe id-then-url, new-blank, close never-zero, select-active), a per-tab dirty flag + markClean(tabId) action, and new-tab seed defaults. The RequestSpec domain model: { method, url, name, params[], headers[], body:{lang,type,text}, auth } where each param/header row is { enabled, key, value, description } and auth is a discriminated union on type ({none}+{bearer,token} in scope). A TabBar strip UI composing the existing Tabs primitive (extended with close ✕, '+' new-tab, dirty marker) and wiring it to the slice. Out of scope: request-editing panels, real send/save, variable interpolation, other auth types, auth→wire-header derivation.

users.value: Two consumer classes. (1) End user: operates the TabBar — open/close/switch request tabs. (2) Sibling tasks consuming the zustand slice programmatically: collection-list opens tabs (open-from-collection), http-engine reads active RequestSpec + calls markClean on successful send, persistence calls markClean on save, request-editing panels bind to the active tab's RequestSpec. T4 owns only the slice + TabBar + the markClean action; the trigger sites live in those sibling tasks (out of scope).

inputs_outputs.value: IN: a RequestSpec payload (from the collection store) for open-from-collection; per-field edits from editing panels via an updateActive(patch) action. OUT (selectors): tabs[] (id + RequestSpec + dirty), activeTabId, the active RequestSpec. OUT (actions): openFromCollection, newTab, closeTab, selectTab, updateActive(patch), markClean(tabId). The slice/domain ALSO owns RequestSpec (de)serialization to/from the persistence JSON shape (RequestSpec is a framework-agnostic serializable domain entity) — but NOT the actual disk read/write nor the markClean trigger, both owned by the persistence task. In-memory at the slice level — no network, no disk I/O here.

integration_points.value: tabsStore in src/renderer/src/lib/ (mirrors settingsStore.ts / toastStore.ts; module-level create<State>, Store suffix, renderer-only — lib/ must NOT import components/). RequestSpec is a framework-agnostic serializable domain entity in the domain layer (not the store). TabBar COMPOSES the existing components/molecules/Tabs.tsx primitive (002) — do NOT re-roll tab a11y/roving-tabindex — and wires it to the store, mounted into Shell.tsx's existing 'tabs' slot above PaneSplit. This EXTENDS the Tabs primitive contract (currently {id,label,badge?,disabled?}, selection-only) and that extension MUST be recorded explicitly in /specify, not assumed: (a) per-tab trailing close ✕ as a SIBLING button, NOT nested in role=tab (nested interactives break roving tabindex); APG keyboard path = Delete on focused tab; (b) a '+' new-tab control placed AFTER the tablist, outside the roving set; (c) dirty indicator via the existing badge? slot or a parallel marker — decided in /specify. open-from-collection is a store action taking a RequestSpec payload; the collection store is out of scope and must NOT be imported.

constraints.value: auth is the SINGLE source of truth for authorization — NEVER seed an Authorization row into headers[] (deriving the wire header is the http-engine's job). auth shape = discriminated union on `type`; T4 defines ONLY {type:'none'} and {type:'bearer',token}, intentionally extensible (other variants owned by auth-panel). New tabs seed auth={type:'bearer',token:'{{apiKey}}'} with literal {{apiKey}} stored verbatim (no interpolation) + a default Accept header row. Never zero tabs. Import/layer boundaries: lib/ slice is renderer-only (no node/electron imports), must NOT import components/, collection store must NOT be imported; RequestSpec stays framework-agnostic in the domain layer; constitution §2.1/§2.3/§3.3/§4 apply. TabBar uses semantic class names from role/tag function; NONE of design/reference.html's generated cruft (data-om-* attrs, __OmT wrappers, inline styles, tweaks-panel) enters the build; colors strictly from design/tokens.json.

non_goals.value: OUT of scope: request-editing panels (params/headers/body/auth editors); real send (http-engine task) and real save (persistence task), plus their markClean trigger sites — T4 ships only the markClean operation, never a real send; bearer-token variable interpolation (the seeded {{ }} literal is stored verbatim here); auth variants beyond {none}+{bearer} and their per-type fields (basic, api-key, oauth2 — owned by auth-panel task); deriving the Authorization wire header from auth (http-engine's job); the collection store itself (T4 consumes a RequestSpec payload, never imports it); reproducing design/reference.html markup or any of its generated cruft. NOTE: RequestSpec (de)serialization shape IS in scope per the I/O dimension — not deferred.

success_criteria.value: Slice unit-tested: every lifecycle action (open-from-collection dedupe id-then-url, new-blank, close never-zero, select-active) + dirty flag + markClean(tabId) covered; invariants asserted (never-zero tabs, dedupe, seed defaults = auth bearer {{apiKey}} verbatim + default Accept header, auth NOT mirrored into headers[]). TabBar renders/selects/closes AND visually matches design/reference.html (look/spacing/states) against design/tokens.json. typecheck + lint + build clean.

edge_cases.value: Lifecycle boundaries: close last/only tab → spawn fresh Untitled GET (never zero); close active tab → select a neighbor; open-from-collection dedupe matches by id first then url → focus existing instead of duplicating. Dirty/markClean races: markClean on non-existent/already-closed tabId = safe no-op; editing a clean tab flips dirty=true; markClean idempotent on unmodified tab; closing a dirty tab → warn/confirm vs silent-drop decided in /specify. Seed/identity integrity: every new tab gets a unique id; {{apiKey}} token + Accept header seeded verbatim (no interpolation); empty/blank-url tab valid; url dedupe normalization (trailing slash, case) defined in /specify. Selection integrity: activeTabId always points at an existing tab after any mutation; removing the active tab never leaves a dangling activeTabId.

references: Postman/Insomnia/Bruno/Hoppscotch — request/tab model concepts (Bruno .bru schema MIT, readable; concepts only, not 1:1 copy)
Existing forge zustand stores — settingsStore.ts / toastStore.ts conventions
RequestSpec as framework-agnostic serializable domain entity living in the domain layer

gaps.description: user-supplied placement guess (confirm via Phase 2 fit-check): implement as a zustand tabs slice + TabBar strip UI, request-editing panels out of scope

intake_classifications.statement: T4: a working-tabs state machine + RequestSpec request data model — each tab holds {method,url,name,params[],headers[],body,auth}; auth is a discriminated union with only {none} and {bearer,token} variants in scope; auth is the single source of truth (no Authorization row in headers[]); tab lifecycle = open-from-collection(dedupe by id then url) / new-blank / close(never zero — last close spawns Untitled GET) / select-active; dirty flag + markClean(tabId) action (triggers out of scope); new tabs seed auth=bearer{{apiKey}} verbatim + a default Accept header; TabBar UI rebuilt with semantic classes from design/reference.html (look/behavior only) + design/tokens.json colors
intake_classifications.minimal_fix: Build ONLY the zustand tabs slice (state + lifecycle actions + dirty/markClean) and the TabBar strip. Define auth union shape with just {none}+{bearer}. Do NOT build: request-editing panels, real send/save, variable interpolation, other auth types, or wire-header derivation from auth.

intake_classifications.statement: Implement as a zustand tabs slice + TabBar strip (placement/mechanism guess)
